task: You are an expert HVAC piping designer and React Three Fiber developer. Focus exclusively on designing and implementing the two pumps and their exact pipe connections in the engine room for the YORK water-cooled chiller 3D simulation. Build detailed, performant 3D components for the Condenser Water Pump (CDWP) and Chilled Water Pump (CHWP) with correct suction and discharge piping sequences, valves, strainers, and instrumentation. Use procedural geometry with @react-three/drei for best performance on Mac M1 Pro.

general.style: Realistic industrial end-suction centrifugal pumps, floor-mounted on concrete base with vibration isolators
general.material: Metallic gray pump body with dark motor housing
general.vfd: Wall-mounted VFD cabinet next to each pump, gray enclosure with yellow 'VFD' label

cdwp_condenser_water_pump.name: CDWP – Condenser Water Pump
cdwp_condenser_water_pump.location: Engine room, positioned near the right side (condenser end) of the YORK chiller

suction_connection.source: Vertical CWS riser descending from rooftop cooling tower basin
sequence: Vertical CWS riser (Dark Green pipe) enters engine room from ceiling
Eccentric reducer if needed (flat side up)
Short straight horizontal pipe run (minimum 6–8 pipe diameters for good flow)
Connects directly to CDWP suction inlet flange

discharge_connection.destination: YORK chiller condenser water inlet (right side of chiller)
sequence: Connects horizontally or with short elbow into chiller condenser inlet

chwp_chilled_water_pump.name: CHWP – Chilled Water Pump
chwp_chilled_water_pump.location: Engine room, positioned near the left side (evaporator end) of the YORK chiller

suction_connection.source: Vertical CHR riser descending from rooftop AHU/RTU
sequence: Vertical CHR riser (Light Blue pipe) enters engine room from ceiling
Short straight horizontal pipe run (minimum 6–8 pipe diameters)
Connects directly to CHWP suction inlet flange

discharge_connection.destination: Vertical CHS riser going up to rooftop AHU/RTU cooling coil
sequence: Connects to vertical CHS riser (Dark Blue) heading upward through roof penetration

visual_and_technical_requirements.modeling: Use procedural geometry: Cylinder for pump body and motor, Box for base, Tube or custom geometry for piping. Keep total polygons low (< 15k per pump assembly).
visual_and_technical_requirements.valves: Gate valves shown with clear handle. Check valves with visible flapper or swing symbol. Y-strainers with removable basket detail and bottom drain.
visual_and_technical_requirements.instruments: Add local pressure gauges (PG) and temperature gauges (TG) on suction and discharge sides of both pumps. Include differential pressure indicator (PDI) across each pump.
visual_and_technical_requirements.labels: Clear text labels: 'CDWP', 'CHWP', pipe labels 'CWS', 'CHR', 'CHS', flow direction arrows.
visual_and_technical_requirements.animation: Subtle pump impeller rotation when running (visible through transparent section or via cutaway). VFD status light.
visual_and_technical_requirements.interactivity: Make pumps, valves, and instruments clickable with tooltips showing component name, tag, and function.

performance_guidelines.target: Optimized for Mac M1 Pro browser – maintain 60 fps. Use instancing where possible. Avoid heavy imported GLB models for pumps and piping.
performance_guidelines.color_standard: Follow 2026 ASHRAE/HVAC conventions: Dark Blue = CHS, Light Blue = CHR, Dark Green = CWS, Light Green = CWR

deliverables: Two separate reusable React Three Fiber components: `<CDWPPumpAssembly />` and `<CHWPPumpAssembly />`
Each component must include: pump model, full suction piping sequence, full discharge piping sequence, valves, strainers, instruments, and proper pipe colors
Clear code comments explaining the connection sequence for CDWP and CHWP
Integration example showing how to place both assemblies relative to the chiller in the engine room scene
output_expectations.verification: Explicitly confirm that the suction and discharge connections for both CDWP and CHWP follow the exact sequences defined in 'pump_design_requirements'. Show flow path from rooftop → pump → chiller (for CDWP) and from AHU → pump → AHU (for CHWP).

notes: This prompt is focused only on pump design and pipe connections in the engine room. The rest of the system (chiller body, rooftop equipment, electrical) will be handled separately. Prioritize clarity, technical accuracy, and performance.
